hyperedges.label: GitHub Pull Request and CI Diagnostic Flow

nodes.label: Direct Main Push Guard
nodes.rationale: Direct pushes to main are blocked so changes pass review and deterministic checks before release.

nodes.label: Local-first Specialist Offload
nodes.rationale: Narrow work and bulky context are delegated to local or self-hosted specialists while the editor remains the primary orchestrator.

nodes.label: Progressive Skill Disclosure
nodes.rationale: Only explicitly attached and allowlisted skills are loaded for each run, reducing context and constraining specialist scope.

nodes.label: Governed Control Plane
nodes.rationale: Policy, signed bundles, bounded graphs, event storage, reports, and dashboards provide auditable governance around specialist execution.

nodes.label: Runtime Skill Attachment
nodes.rationale: Invocations must select skills from each agent's allowlist instead of loading the complete skill library.

nodes.label: Bounded Runtime Plugin Evidence
nodes.rationale: Declared tools collect bounded read-only evidence before model execution and return attributable evidence artifacts.

nodes.label: Read-only Argo Diagnostics
nodes.rationale: Argo syncs and workflow reruns require explicit approval, and Argo-layer failures must be distinguished from generic Kubernetes failures.

nodes.label: Bounded Vanilla Frontend Scope
nodes.rationale: The specialist stays within requested HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and README files and avoids frameworks unless requested.

nodes.label: Evidence-backed GitHub Diagnostics
nodes.rationale: Repository diagnosis must cite attempted commands and concrete identifiers and must not claim write actions.

nodes.label: Bounded Go Helpers
nodes.rationale: Each invocation should produce one function or a small cohesive group without unrelated refactoring or unjustified dependencies.

nodes.label: Bounded Go Scaffolding
nodes.rationale: Scaffolding is constrained to the described package and API and returns snippets rather than rewriting the repository.

nodes.label: MCP-backed Linear Mutation Evidence
nodes.rationale: Linear mutations cannot be reported as completed without attached downstream MCP tool evidence and explicit approval handling.

nodes.label: Documentation Citation Requirements
nodes.rationale: Material recommendations require credible sources, version context, and explicit unresolved gaps.

nodes.label: Shared Agent Rules
nodes.rationale: All specialists remain within attached skills, report uncertainty, avoid fabricated evidence, and defer repository mutation until audited write support exists.

nodes.label: Argo Diagnostic Safety
nodes.rationale: Inspection is permitted, but syncs and reruns require approval and incomplete evidence must remain visible.

nodes.label: Suggestion-only Frontend Output
nodes.rationale: The frontend specialist returns JSON code snippets and does not directly apply repository changes.

nodes.label: Read-only Repository Diagnostics
nodes.rationale: GitHub inspection is evidence-oriented and excludes direct repository or issue mutation.

nodes.label: Compact Go Helper Output
nodes.rationale: Small idiomatic helpers are returned in a compact JSON envelope while full code remains isolated in code artifacts.

nodes.label: Suggestion-only Implementation Changes
nodes.rationale: The initial implementer drafts focused patches but leaves repository writes to the orchestrator until audited write support exists.

nodes.label: Read-only Cluster Diagnostics
nodes.rationale: Cluster resources may be inspected and safe checks suggested, but destructive mutations require explicit approval.

nodes.label: Auditable MCP Mutations
nodes.rationale: Proposed, executed, and blocked Linear mutations are separated so only MCP-evidenced actions are reported as completed.

nodes.label: Bounded Task Decomposition
nodes.rationale: Planning is limited to the supplied task and context, exposes uncertainty, and does not perform edits or broad product strategy.

nodes.label: Researcher Agent
nodes.rationale: Produces concise evidence-backed findings without making final architecture decisions or mutating code.

nodes.label: Reviewer Agent
nodes.rationale: Prioritizes correctness, safety, maintainability, and concrete validation risks while remaining read-only.

nodes.label: Test Designer Agent
nodes.rationale: Maps change risks to focused unit, integration, golden, CLI, and manual validation.

nodes.label: Web and Documentation Search Agent
nodes.rationale: Retrieves targeted documentation and returns cited findings without making final design decisions.

nodes.label: Governed Specialist Execution
nodes.rationale: The editor remains the orchestrator while Prism owns policy-governed specialist execution.

nodes.label: Flat Delegated Synthesis Context
nodes.rationale: Delegated orchestrator input remains nearly flat because local agents return compact summaries while baseline context grows.

nodes.label: Orchestrator Context Quality
nodes.rationale: Moving narrow evidence-heavy work to specialists reduces both paid token load and attention dilution in the primary session.

nodes.label: Editor-Orchestrated Local Specialists
nodes.rationale: Prism combines editor-side orchestration, MCP, local specialists, explicit skills, and executable savings benchmarks.

nodes.label: Per-Invocation Skill Progressive Disclosure
nodes.rationale: Each run loads only explicitly requested and allowlisted skills to prevent scope creep and context bloat.

nodes.label: Shared Core and Thin Adapters
nodes.rationale: CLI and MCP should reuse one runtime and response contract instead of duplicating behavior.

nodes.label: Stable Prompt Prefix Layout
nodes.rationale: Stable instructions precede volatile evidence to improve prefix-cache reuse on serving runtimes such as SGLang.

nodes.label: Metadata-First Observability
nodes.rationale: Run metadata should be observable without storing raw prompts, logs, or evidence by default.

nodes.label: Structural Safety Controls
nodes.rationale: Safety is enforced through allowlists, bounded plugins, policy, budgets, signatures, approvals, and fail-closed validation.

nodes.label: Deterministic Explainable Controls
nodes.rationale: Routers, policies, validators, and graph execution should begin deterministic and explainable before model-based classification is added.

nodes.label: Executable Reproducible Benchmarking
nodes.rationale: Token, cost, quality, and latency claims must be generated from repeatable scenarios rather than presentation estimates.

nodes.label: Specialist Context Compression
nodes.rationale: Specialists process raw context locally and return compact high-signal envelopes instead of forwarding evidence to the orchestrator.

nodes.label: Nearly Flat Orchestrator Context
nodes.rationale: Orchestrator input grows with the number of summaries rather than the total raw evidence volume.

nodes.label: Skill Progressive Disclosure
nodes.rationale: The runtime discovers skill metadata broadly but injects full instructions only for explicitly attached skills.

nodes.label: Argo Sync Read-Only Guardrail
nodes.rationale: Diagnosis may inspect application drift and Kubernetes evidence but must not trigger synchronization unless explicitly requested.

nodes.label: Argo Workflow Mutation Guardrail
nodes.rationale: Workflow diagnosis must not terminate, resubmit, or rerun workflows unless explicitly requested.

nodes.label: Failed Workflow Evidence Collection
nodes.rationale: Diagnosis should begin with bounded workflow, node, log, and evidence collection.

nodes.label: Authoritative Source Harvest
nodes.rationale: Official, version-matched documentation reduces unsupported or incompatible implementation guidance.

nodes.label: Insufficient Evidence Callback
nodes.rationale: Missing product, version, or scope identifiers must be resolved before final recommendations are made.

nodes.label: Deterministic Todo Persistence
nodes.rationale: A stable storage key and consistent hydration make browser-native todo state reproducible.

nodes.label: Static Frontend Local Testing
nodes.rationale: Short copy-paste instructions should let users run and verify a static application without build tooling.

nodes.label: Accessible Dependency-Free SPA
nodes.rationale: Semantic controls, explicit UI states, and no external dependencies keep the interface portable and browser-native.

nodes.label: GitHub Actions Failure Diagnosis
nodes.rationale: Runs, jobs, failing steps, logs, and recurrence signals distinguish transient failures from deterministic or configuration failures.

nodes.label: Approval-Gated Workflow Reruns
nodes.rationale: Workflow reruns and dispatches change external state and therefore require explicit approval.

nodes.label: Pull Request Merge Readiness
nodes.rationale: Merge readiness depends jointly on identity, checks, reviews, mergeability, and risky changed files.

nodes.label: Read-Only Pull Request Triage
nodes.rationale: Triage gathers evidence but must not merge, approve, or edit pull-request state.

nodes.label: Focused Go Helper Implementation
nodes.rationale: Reading signatures, callers, and package conventions supports a bounded helper implementation without unrelated refactoring.

nodes.label: Pure Helper Preference
nodes.rationale: Pure helpers reduce side effects and keep utility behavior predictable.

nodes.label: Effective Go Naming and Comment Style
nodes.norm_label: effective go naming and comment style

nodes.label: No Implicit go.mod Changes
nodes.rationale: Module-level dependency changes require explicit caller authorization.

nodes.label: Complexity and Allocation Tradeoffs
nodes.rationale: Non-trivial utilities should disclose computational and allocation costs.

nodes.label: Compact Table-Driven Examples
nodes.rationale: Examples should be table-driven only when they fit the available context budget.

nodes.label: Deterministic Test Boundaries
nodes.rationale: Clock, network, and filesystem dependencies require supplied mock seams to preserve deterministic tests.

nodes.label: Read-Only Rollout Guardrail
nodes.rationale: Rollback, restart, and apply operations require an explicit request.

nodes.label: Read-Only Triage Guardrail
nodes.rationale: Delete, patch, restart, and exec operations are excluded from default incident triage.

nodes.label: Search Before Linear Mutation
nodes.rationale: Triage searches for duplicates and current state before proposing workflow changes.

nodes.label: Linear Write Evidence Guardrail
nodes.rationale: A write may be reported as executed only when an MCP response artifact proves it.

nodes.label: Fail-Closed Evidence Gate
nodes.rationale: Specialists are not run until minimum domain identifiers are available.

nodes.label: First-Party Source Precedence
nodes.rationale: Tool-specific investigations should prefer vendor-authored skills and documentation.

nodes.label: Official Release Note Coverage
nodes.rationale: Complete official coverage is required to identify breaking changes reliably and determine confidence.

nodes.label: Fixture Isolation Policy
nodes.rationale: Temporary miniature fixtures are preferred unless stable committed fixtures must differ from production examples.

nodes.label: Subscription Pricing Comparability Limit
nodes.rationale: Seat subscriptions with included usage and overages are not directly comparable to per-token API rates.

nodes.label: Read-Time Default Before Backfill
nodes.rationale: Serving a default profile at read time provides compatibility before asynchronous persistence of defaults.

nodes.label: Critical Notification Bypass
nodes.rationale: Incident and high-severity security notifications bypass digest or quiet hours to preserve critical visibility.

nodes.label: Pure Deterministic Validation
nodes.rationale: Avoiding database and network calls makes validation deterministic and directly testable with table-driven unit tests.

nodes.label: Notification Preferences Implementation Plan
nodes.rationale: Scoped package boundaries, a stable API contract, read-time defaults, and asynchronous migration reduce rollout risk while aligning UX and backend behavior.

nodes.label: Read Defaults and Migration Backfill
nodes.rationale: Read-time defaults are introduced before asynchronous backfill to minimize migration risk.

nodes.label: Deterministic Notification Preference Validation
nodes.rationale: Sorted keyed validation errors create stable UI mappings and deterministic table-driven tests.

nodes.label: Focused Specialist Delegation Model
nodes.rationale: Prism delegates focused documentation, scaffold, validation, and testing tasks and synthesizes compact summaries instead of loading broad context into one orchestrator.

nodes.label: Monolithic Incident Context Inflation
nodes.rationale: Repeated history, pasted evidence, skills, constitutions, and adjacent coding requests inflate the baseline orchestrator context before report generation.

nodes.label: Payments Metrics and API Migration Policy
nodes.norm_label: payments metrics and api migration policy

nodes.label: GitOps-First Recovery Policy
nodes.rationale: Production-adjacent homelab recovery should capture Argo diffs and prefer Argo CD synchronization over direct kubectl mutation.

nodes.label: Argo CD Sync Wave Health Requirement
nodes.norm_label: argo cd sync wave health requirement

nodes.label: Failed Argo Sync State
nodes.rationale: The desired metrics-port-v2 manifest was not applied, leaving the application OutOfSync and Degraded.

nodes.label: ETL API v2 Requirement
nodes.rationale: The extract template exits because platform v2.4.0 requires payments.apiVersion v2.

nodes.label: kubectl apply Failure
nodes.rationale: The deployment exceeded its progress deadline with only one of three replicas ready.

nodes.label: PR #42 Merge Blockers
nodes.rationale: The deploy-homelab check failed and platform-bot requested changes because payments-api was OutOfSync.

nodes.label: Unknown metrics-port-v2 Flag
nodes.rationale: The container exits with status 1 because it rejects the new metrics-port-v2 flag.

nodes.label: Upgrade Configuration Mismatch
nodes.rationale: Platform v2.4.0 breaking changes were merged without updating all deployment configuration and workflow consumers.

nodes.label: Incident Recovery Sequence
nodes.rationale: Update deployment and ETL configuration, re-sync Argo, restore cluster health, then re-run PR CI.

nodes.label: Minimal Vanilla Architecture
nodes.rationale: Keep the demo dead simple, avoid React/Vite and animations, and prioritize clarity and correctness.

nodes.label: Stable Todo IDs
nodes.rationale: Timestamp plus random identifiers are considered sufficiently stable for this demo.

nodes.label: Storage and Rendering Separation
nodes.rationale: Storage and rendering concerns should be isolated while functions remain small and focused.

nodes.label: Read-Only Incident Offload
nodes.rationale: The starter bundle is constrained to read-only Kubernetes incident investigation.

nodes.label: Sensitive Request Denials
nodes.rationale: Write actions, oversized evidence, raw prompt capture, and remote model runtimes are denied to preserve the read-only and privacy boundary.

nodes.label: Delegation
nodes.rationale: The visible tagline positions delegation as the primary value Prism adds to an AI editor.

nodes.label: Prismatic Routing
nodes.rationale: The central prism transforms incoming monochrome streams into separated colored capability paths, visually explaining routing and specialization.

nodes.label: Before Prism: One Premium Prompt Carries Task and All Context
nodes.norm_label: before prism: one premium prompt carries task and all context

nodes.label: After Prism: Editor Orchestrates and Delegates via MCP
nodes.norm_label: after prism: editor orchestrates and delegates via mcp

nodes.label: Live Coding Benchmark: 6,191 to 363 Orchestrator Input Tokens, 94.1% Reduction
nodes.norm_label: live coding benchmark: 6,191 to 363 orchestrator input tokens, 94.1% reduction

nodes.label: Step 1 Initial Request (~120 tokens to orchestrator)
nodes.norm_label: step 1 initial request (~120 tokens to orchestrator)

nodes.label: Step 3 Prism Fans Out via MCP
nodes.norm_label: step 3 prism fans out via mcp

nodes.label: Specialist A Skill and Evidence (~2,080 tokens)
nodes.norm_label: specialist a skill and evidence (~2,080 tokens)

nodes.label: Specialist B Skill and Evidence (~2,080 tokens)
nodes.norm_label: specialist b skill and evidence (~2,080 tokens)

nodes.label: Specialist C Skill and Evidence (~2,080 tokens)
nodes.norm_label: specialist c skill and evidence (~2,080 tokens)

nodes.label: Step 4 Orchestrator Synthesizes Brief and Three Summaries (363 tokens)
nodes.norm_label: step 4 orchestrator synthesizes brief and three summaries (363 tokens)

nodes.label: Step 5 Final Response with Same Quality Rubric
nodes.norm_label: step 5 final response with same quality rubric

nodes.label: 94.1% Orchestrator Input Reduction
nodes.rationale: Prism keeps raw skill and evidence context inside delegated specialist work, then returns compact summaries to the orchestrator.

nodes.label: Localized Context Loading
nodes.rationale: Each specialist receives its own skill and evidence context, while the orchestrator receives only the initial brief and compressed specialist outputs.

nodes.label: Quality-Preserving Token Efficiency
nodes.rationale: The workflow claims a large reduction in orchestrator input tokens while retaining the same final-response quality rubric.

hyperedges.label: GitHub Pull Request and CI Diagnostic Flow
